title: Gut-Brain Connection Tracker
description: Track daily connections between your gut health, diet, and mood. Research suggests 2-week tracking reveals meaningful patterns between dietary habits and mental health outcomes.
instructions: Complete this log daily, ideally in the evening. Rate your gut health, mood, and note what you ate. Over 2+ weeks, patterns will emerge showing how your gut and brain communicate.

fields.label: Did you experience brain fog today?
How many servings of fiber-rich foods did you eat?
Did you eat fermented foods today?
Observations about gut-mood connections today
fields.placeholder: e.g., Noticed mood dipped after fast food lunch, felt better after evening walk

disclaimer: This tracking log is an educational self-monitoring tool. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice. If tracking your food intake causes anxiety or disordered eating patterns, please stop and consult a healthcare provider. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
